'''Alex Walmsley''' (born 10 April 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] and has spent time on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at his former club in the second tier.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Walmsley was born in [[Dewsbury]], West Yorkshire, England.

==Club career==
Walmsley started his career in the amateur leagues, playing for [[Dewsbury Celtic]], and his university team, [[Leeds Metropolitan University|Leeds Met Carnegie]].<ref>{{cite web|title=The stars of 2013: The talents that will hit the big time next year|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/others/the-stars-of-2013-the-talents-that-will-hit-the-big-time-next-year-8432844.html?action=gallery&ino=5|work=The Independent|date = 2 January 2013|access-date=23 February 2014}}</ref> In 2011, he received the [[National Conference League]] player of the year award.<ref>{{cite web|title=Top Conference award for Alex|url=http://www.batleynews.co.uk/sport/local-sport/top-conference-award-for-alex-1-3821938|work=Batley and Birstall News|publisher=Johnston Press|access-date=23 February 2014|date=30 September 2011}}</ref>

In February 2012, Walmsley signed for [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]] following a spell on trial at the club.<ref>{{cite web|title=Batley Bulldogs complete Walmsley deal|url=http://www.batleynews.co.uk/sport/local-sport/batley-bulldogs-complete-walmsley-deal-1-4217325|work=Batley and Birstall News|publisher=Johnston Press|access-date=23 February 2014|date=6 February 2012}}</ref> He won Batley's player of the year award and the Championship Young Player of the Year during his only season with the club, before signing with [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] for the start of the 2013 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19585081|title=St Helens sign Batley Bulldogs prop Alex Walmsley|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=13 September 2012|access-date= 29 March 2013}}</ref>

Walmsley briefly returned to Batley at the start of 2013 on a [[Super League#Dual registration|dual registration]] before establishing himself in the St Helens first team that season.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.examiner.co.uk/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-news/2013/01/10/new-st-helens-super-league-signing-alex-walmsley-hoping-for-big-start-against-huddersfield-giants-86081-32577434|title=New St Helens Super League signing Alex Walmsley hoping for big start against Huddersfield Giants|newspaper=Huddersfield Examiner|date= 10 January 2013|access-date=29 March 2013}}</ref>

St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and Walmsley was selected to play from the interchange bench in their 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref>

Walmsley's form in 2015 saw him named to the [[Super League Dream Team]] for the first time as well as earning him a nomination for the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] award.<ref>[http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/article/480/10002841/adam-cuthbertson-zak-hardaker-and-alex-walmsley-up-for-man-of-steel Trio up for Man of Steel award]</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|work=Daily Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

Walmsley played in St Helens 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 17 July 2021, he played for St. Helens in their 26-12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref>
On 9 October 2021, he played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].  It was the club's third successive championship victory in a row.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 13 September 2022, Walmsley was ruled out for the remainder of the 2022 Super League season and the World Cup with a foot injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-league/alex-walmsley-injury-world-cup-2022-b2166073.html/|title=England prop Alex Walmsley ruled out of World Cup|website=www.independent.co.uk}}</ref>
On 18 February 2023, Walmsley played in St Helens 13-12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
In round 2 of the 2023 Super League season, Walmsley scored two tries for St Helens in a 24-6 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/feb/26/castleford-st-helens-super-league-rugby-league-match-report/|title=World champions St Helens comfortably sweep Castleford aside in opener|newspaper=The Guardian|date=26 February 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
In the 2023 Challenge Cup semi-final, Walmsley suffered a knee injury following a cannonball tackle from Leigh player [[John Asiata]]. St Helens would go on to lose the match 12-10 and Walmsley was later ruled out for 12 weeks.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66301945|title=Paul Wellens: St Helens boss accuses RFL of failing to protect players after Leigh cup loss|work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/st-helens-duo-out-for-the-season-after-incredibly-dangerous-tackles-79048/|title=St Helens duo out for the season after "incredibly dangerous" tackles|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=25 July 2023 }}</ref>
Walmsley played 18 games for St Helens in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished third on the table. He played in St Helens narrow loss against the Catalans Dragons in the semi-final which stopped them reaching a fifth successive grand final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/23856044.paul-wellens-q-saints-review-year-look-2024/|title=Paul Wellens Q&A: Saints review of year and a look to 2024|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk|date=14 October 2023 }}</ref>
Walmsley played 13 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
‘Soul destroying’: Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
Walmsley played 28 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs 20-12 semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

Walmsley played for [[England]]
In the 2025 [[Rugby League Ashes]] series . Playing from the bench - some say he should have started with the [[Australians]] having difficulty bringing him down and containing his offload.

==International career==
Walmsley was a member of the [[England national rugby league team|England]] team which played in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|work=Daily Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref> In the final against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], Walmsley came off the bench in England's 6–0 defeat.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/dec/02/rugby-league-world-cup-australia-beat-england-in-the-closest-final-in-decades|title=Australia beat England in closest Rugby League World Cup final in decades|website=[[TheGuardian.com]]|date=2 December 2017}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/2017-rugby-league-world-cup-final-australia-england-teams-players-times-dates-venues-streaming-watch-television-broadcast/v9io7j93safi1c1djdw4kw57b|title = 2017 Rugby League World Cup final: When is it, who's involved, and how to watch?| date=28 November 2017 }}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|work=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|work=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
